channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: T1 Threatens Legal Action over Player Harassment and Privacy Violations
item.content:encoded:
T1 has issued statements that threaten “formal legal action” over online harassment of its players and violations of player privacy. The organization asked for fans’ cooperation in “fostering a healthy and respectful fan culture.”

The statements follow the legal action brought by the player agency, Fanable, who represent esports players, including Lee ‘Faker’ Sang-hyeok and Lee ‘Gumayusi’ Min-hyeong.

T1 statements issue a stern warning over player harassment

T1 issued two statements over social media, warning of legal action against different types of harassment, with the first statement focusing on player privacy.

The Korean organization stated, “Recently, there have been numerous recorded instances of privacy infringement against our players, including waiting outside their team house for unauthorized filming as they head to our HQ.”

T1’s headquarters has in the past reportedly been the target of protesting fans, including hired trucks to block the building’s access, with staff and players reportedly receiving funeral wreaths.

The organization added that actions that take place outside of the players’ working schedule “cannot be justified under any circumstances.”

The statement then threatens legal action, stating, “Should these violations continue, we will take firm measures, including legal action, to ensure the safety and protect the rights of our players.”

The organization asked, “for the cooperation of all fans in fostering a healthy and respectful fan culture.”

A second statement, the following day, targeted online harassment, stating, “We are closely monitoring online communities and social media platforms for the creation of malicious rumors, the dissemination of false information, and posts containing personal attacks, defamation, or mockery directed at our players.”

Adding, “Actions that damage the reputation of our players and cause psychological distress may result in civil and criminal liability under relevant laws and regulations… In cases where malicious behavior is repetitive or severe, we intend to respond strictly, which may include taking formal legal action.”

T1 statements follow Faker and Gumayusi legal action

The statements closely follow news from Korea that the agency Fanable would take legal action to protect its athletes from online harassment.

The agency stated, “The creation, distribution, and defamation of malicious posts regarding our athletes in some communities have continued at a level that exceeds acceptable limits without any effort at self-regulation.”

Adding, “We have reached the conclusion that we can no longer overlook this situation,” before confirming both civil and criminal action.

Gumayusi was the victim of high-profile harassment last year, with Korean lawmaker Jeon Yong-gi calling for more robust action by Korea e-Sports Association (KeSPA), Riot Games, and orgs to tackle online abuse.

The lawmaker directly attacked T1 fan group T1 Support Gallery (aka T1 Gall), stating, “I deeply regret the behaviour of some community users, such as the ‘T1 Support Gallery’, who have been cyberbullying [Gumayusi] for a long time, and I urge them to stop immediately.” (translated)

Does more need to be done to protect players? Discuss that on our Discord.
For more League of Legends news, see why Sheo suddenly departed Team Heretics.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

item.title: NYT Connections Hints Today (#1040)—Answers for Thursday, April 16
item.content:encoded:
How’s that NYT Connections streak looking? Are you managing a perfect record throughout this week? If not, let’s right that wrong, and if you are, then let’s keep pushing! I’ve got today’s NYT Connections hints and answers in this guide, so you can get the solution in place as quickly as possible.

Scroll down to get the NYT Connections help you might be looking for!

How to Play NYT Connections

In June 2023, NYT Connections emerged as yet another game in the ever-growing lineup of the New York Times titles. This title soared immediately, and it’s easy to see why. As a free-to-play, accessible New York Times game, NYT Connections is available worldwide and can be played on desktop devices or in a mobile app.

But how do you play NYT Connections?

The NYT team will provide you with a grid of 16 words once a day. The only task left up to you is to sort those words into four groups of four by finding their commonalities. Every NYT Connections puzzle has just one solution, and some words can have double meanings, so it can sometimes be tricky to figure out the answers.

It’s also important to stress that the words are color-coded: yellow, blue, green, and purple. That’s in order of difficulty, from least to most.

What’s critical here is that you get four guesses every day, and if you use those, your NYT Connections answers for today will be out of reach, as it’ll be game over.

Today’s NYT Connections Hints (April 16)

Here are the words from today’s NYT Connections puzzle:

  • COOL
  • WHIP
  • MASK
  • RIDE
  • NEEDLE
  • CAMP
  • FAN
  • WHEELS
  • AUTO
  • BODYSUIT
  • HEAT
  • ROAST
  • DAY
  • CLAWS
  • BRA
  • RIB

And here are the hints I can offer for today’s NYT Connections:

  • Yellow – Words you can use in place of ‘tease’
  • Green – Words you might find on a thermostat
  • Blue – Items you’d find in Catwoman’s closet
  • Purple – Terms that follow ‘training’

Here’s the final warning before we get to the NYT Connections answers for today, April 16:

Are you sure you want to scroll further?

Very well!

NYT Connections Answers Explained for Today, April 16 (#1040)

If you want to jump straight to the answers for today’s NYT Connections puzzle, I’ve got you covered:

  • Yellow – NEEDLE, RIB, RIDE, ROAST
  • Green – AUTO, COOL, FAN, HEAT
  • Blue – BODYSUIT, CLAWS, MASK, WHIP
  • Purple – BRA, CAMP, DAY, WHEELS

When Does NYT Connections Reset?

That’s today’s puzzle, but if you want the next one, the game resets daily at midnight local time (that’s your local time). You’ll have 24 hours to complete the puzzle when midnight ticks over in your timezone.

How did you do? Did you get a perfect run? Let me know what your streak looks like on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Beast of Reincarnation Pre-Order Details and Game Editions Revealed
item.content:encoded:
A reliable insider has stated that Beast of Reincarnation pre-orders should begin today and has revealed pricing, Deluxe edition content, and more.

For those unaware, the developers behind the iconic Pokémon games, Game Freak, announced that they were venturing into a brand new realm with their new title, Beast of Reincarnation. The developers recently announced that the souls-like RPG should release this August, and it seems players can begin pre-ordering the game today.

Beast of Reincarnation Pre-Orders ‘Should’ Begin Today

Reliable insider billbil-kun recently took to X (formerly Twitter) to say that Beast of Reincarnation should kick off today, April 16. The US pricing for the title was also revealed, with the standard edition costing $59.99 and the deluxe edition priced at $69.99. Players who pre-order the title will receive the Brown Shiba Skin for Koo and 30,000 Amber, the in-game currency.

Coming to the deluxe edition content, the post reveals that it will feature Black & Brown Shiba Skins for Koo, “Oni’s Hat” for Emma, Emma’s sword “Big Dipper”, 100,000 Amber (in-game currency), and several vegetable seedlings. The title will also receive a physical release, with PS5 users receiving physical copies of both the standard and deluxe editions. In contrast, Xbox users only get the option for the standard edition.

“Beast of Reincarnation” is set in a post-apocalyptic Japan, offering players a unique “one-person, one-dog action RPG” experience. The story follows Emma, an outcast shunned by society because of her affliction, and her companion, Koo, a blighted dog. Players will journey through this world, afflicted by blight, with Koo by their side, encountering various challenges and uncovering dangerous truths. Along the way, they will face formidable bosses whose powers they must capture.

In other news, Kota Furushima recently addressed long-standing Game Freak criticism while discussing Beast of Reincarnation. What are your thoughts on Beast of Reincarnation pre-order and game edition details? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Windrose hitting 500,000 players within 2 days of early access. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Windrose Hits 500,000 Players As Positive Reviews Soar in Early Access
item.content:encoded:
The team at Kraken Express has revealed how Windrose, the open-world pirate survival game, has managed to hit half a million players in just two days of early access. The game dropped on April 14, emerging to strong reviews on account of all the meat on the game’s bones, the charming visuals, and the massive open-world environment ripe for exploration.

Windrose was onto something solid in the earliest days of its playtests, and the recent demo that went live during Steam Next Fest was one of the most popular on the entire platform.

Windrose Sets Sail and Dominates the Seas

The pirating space has been dipped into aplenty over the years, with games like Black Flag, Sea of Thieves, Greedfall, and Skull & Bones all offering their take on an open-ocean, pirate theme. Windrose has taken the concept and turned it into a PvE survival title with heaps of customization, construction, and intense combat sequences.

Windrose, formerly known as Crosswind, gives players a vast world to dig into right out of the gate. Players are thrown into the deep end after suffering a shipwreck, and it’s up to them to rebuild their pirating empire, starting with little more than sticks and stones.

Before long, you’ll find yourself at the helm of a hulking ship, mastering a crew, outposts, and troubling anyone who challenges you on the open seas. There isn’t any PvP in Windrose yet, but it’s early days, so watch this space.

In a post on social media, Kraken Express revealed that a whopping half a million players have entered Windrose in just two days of early access:

What makes this more impressive is that Windrose isn’t just another free-to-play survival title. It has a fairly decent price tag of around $26.99, which means Kraken Express has already made bank. The studio, backed by Pocketpair Publishing, hasn’t made anything as a collective before Windrose, making the success even more admirable.

On Steam, Windrose has managed to generate a peak concurrent player count of more than 97,000 users, and the slew of reviews stitched together by users is reflecting in 85% positivity, which is nuts for a brand-new title in the early access space.

Have you played Windrose? Let us know if you’re enjoying it on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that PIONER has a new 2026 roadmap

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: Fortnite v40.20 April 16 Update—Complete Patch Notes
item.content:encoded:
If you’ve come for all the Fortnite v40.20 patch notes in the April 16 update, you’re in the right place. We’ve got news of gameplay fixes, visual improvements, and changes to the Battle Pass and Quests.

As with every week, everyone was waiting for the Fortnite Servers to be back up after the April 16 downtime. While you were doing this, Epic was making some adjustments to the game, which should improve the experience overall.

All Fortnite v40.20 Patch Notes For April 16 Update

The initial patch notes were leaked courtesy of FireMonkey, and here’s what has been reported.

“GENERAL FIXES & IMPROVEMENTS

This update comes with a large number of bug fixes across all of Fortnite. Here are a few notable ones we’ve seen reported in community spaces:

Gameplay

  • Chaos Reloader Shotgun now reloads correctly while unequipped, displays reload progress in the Quick Bar, and fires consistently. 
  • Thorne’s Vampiric Blade no longer causes the jump input to become unresponsive after using its secondary attack.
  • Port-a-Bunkers and Shield Bubbles are now available in Ranked Zero Build as intended.
  • Your arrow will no longer incorrectly appear below teammates’ arrows on the minimap.
  • Returning to Fortnite after Alt-Tabbing or pressing the Windows key no longer incorrectly opens your inventory when in a match. 

Cosmetics & Visuals

  • Fixed an issue causing some Outfits and cosmetics to appear shinier or darker than intended on higher quality settings.
  • Fixed visual and animation physics issues affecting certain Outfits on Performance Mode or lower-end devices.
  • The Saitama Outfit’s cape now loads its textures correctly.
  • The Bugs Bunny Outfit’s eyes no longer appear broken when previewing Pickaxes and Back Blings in the Locker.
  • Darkfire Bundle Weapon Wraps now display their correct individual glow colors instead of all appearing purple.
  • Fixed an issue where some players’ profiles could incorrectly display the default Outfit.
  • Cosmetic previews now display cleanly without a persisting loading spinner in the Shop and Locker.

Battle Pass & Quests

  • Fixed an issue where the Battle Pass could show an incorrect unlock notification when previewing a different reward set. 
  • Daily Quests now display the correct refresh time and no longer reset earlier than stated.

Fortnite OG

  • Item icons no longer appear stretched on Vending Machines.
  • Players can now manage their Quick Bar inventory without issues after using a Pirate Cannon.
  • The Reboot Van at Paradise Palms is no longer missing.
  • Daily Quests no longer reference locations that have been removed from the current map rotation.

Other Modes & Platforms

  • [Reload] Ranked matches no longer display the “Placeholder Playlist” text on the Starter Island.
  • [LEGO Fortnite] Fixed an issue where the World Restore option would give an error when attempting to restore to a previous checkpoint.
  • [Mobile] Fixed visual issues affecting gameplay for some players on Google Pixel 10 devices.
  • [Mobile] The Emote Wheel is no longer too small and difficult to read on certain devices.
  • For Save the World fixes and improvements, check out this Communities Post.

We’re continuing to monitor your reports on social media and in community spaces, please keep them coming and we’ll work to fix them.”

What do you make of the newest Fortnite update? Is there anything Epic keeps overlooking each week? Let us know your gripes and feedback through the Insider Gaming Discord.

We have much more Fortnite news here, including word on a Fortnite & The Boys coming together for a heroic collaboration, and Fortnite adding AI-Powered NPCs.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton
